text: fat is 1.8g. saturated fat, omega3, and polyunsaturated fat are doing most of the work. for a legume, fibre and practicality are supposed to carry the case.
carbs is 4g. fibre is where things start to fall off. for legumes, the carbs look much better when fibre and protein are both backing them up.
protein is 3.3g. protein quality is a real strength here. protein support is a big part of what makes legumes worth it.
vitamin e at 0% DV, vitamin c at 0% DV stand out most. that is barely moving the needle.
minerals are basically not part of the case here.

on the plus side. filling for the calories because legumes usually earn a lot of practical value from satiety. cheap staple with real payoff because this matters because a food is stronger when people can actually use it often. fibre-forward category identity because legumes are supposed to bring stable carbs and fibre.

now the downsides. dilution makes it less impressive than whole legumes because the legume identity is still there, but not in full strength. can take planning or prep because that is a small but real friction cost. protein quality still has a ceiling because good, but not meat or dairy level.
C tier. Best only when convenience outweighs the weaker fibre or protein payoff.
